changes_summary: Revised the plan to add an explicit overlay-identity bridge for existing caller shapes, and expanded the migration checklist to cover popup-marker-dependent helpers like `elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` without widening M2 beyond the overlay ownership subsystem.

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/overlayStack.ts` exists, exports JSDoc-documented public actions/selectors for overlay registration, topmost queries, stack-derived layering, and element/topmost bridge helpers, and is the only automated source of truth for modal body pointer-events and focus restoration.

An explicit overlay-identity bridge exists under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` so existing caller shapes can consume topmost state without bespoke prop threading: ref-based dialog callers and callback-only lightbox hooks both have a sanctioned migration path.

No raw `@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card)` imports remain anywhere under `src/` outside `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/`, including type-only imports that would fail the invariant grep.

`LightboxShell.tsx` no longer imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` directly and instead consumes a sanctioned overlay-aware wrapper path under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` that preserves its current modal, dismissal-cancellation, custom event-handler contract, and `data-dialog-backdrop` / `data-lightbox-popup` marker exposure.
The existing Base UI `preview-card` portal wrapper is migrated into the centralized overlay ownership layer so no in-repo Base UI portal primitive with stack-relevant ownership remains outside M2.

DOM/z-index precedence helpers are retired for migrated overlay flows: `uiLayers.ts`, lightbox interaction/navigation hooks, and `LoraSelectorModal` no longer determine topmost state by querying DOM markers and comparing computed z-index values.

Helper-based container and popup-overlay resolution is centralized and preserved: the known callers in `CommunityLorasTab.tsx`, `MyLorasTab.tsx`, `sharedSections.tsx`, `useSlidingPane.ts`, `elementPolicy.ts`, and `useMobileInteractions.ts` use shared overlay helpers instead of open-coded marker queries.

Backdrop-marker and popup-marker compatibility are explicit and intact during M2: centralized wrappers continue exposing the required markers, and the known live consumers in `useLightboxTransition.ts`, `src/styles/components/interactions.css`, `src/index.css`, `elementPolicy.ts`, and `useMobileInteractions.ts` are preserved or migrated in the same change.

Caller-side layering escape hatches for migrated overlay primitives are removed or neutralized: no remaining `zIndexBase`, overlay z-index class overrides, or equivalent caller-owned numeric layering controls are needed for Dialog, Select, Popover, Menu, Tooltip, AlertDialog, or HoverCard flows.
Integration tests cover `open A -> open B -> close A -> close B` for both Dialog+Popover and Menu+Dialog, and assert `document.body.style.pointerEvents === ''` after full close.
Tests validate the overlay identity bridge and replace the old DOM-z-index ordering strategy, including the lightbox precedence path and the lightbox-transition cleanup contract.
`npm test` passes after the overlay migration.
Public wrapper entrypoints remain import-compatible for existing consumers where possible, while removing only the layering-specific escape hatches that conflict with central ownership.

The overlay stack implementation remains a small, dedicated ordered-store abstraction plus narrow bridge helpers rather than absorbing unrelated persistence, styling, or feature-level state responsibilities.

Deferred manual checks pass in a real app session: nested LoRA modal plus Model Select closes cleanly, drag interactions do not spuriously dismiss overlays, and focus trap / Escape / restore behavior feels correct.

assumptions: The critique points to missing implementation detail, not a wrong root-cause hypothesis: M2 should still centralize Base UI portal ownership rather than pivot to a different subsystem.
A context/ref-based overlay identity bridge is the narrowest way to migrate existing caller shapes without broad feature-level prop churn.

Preserving `data-dialog-content`, `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-lightbox-popup`, and the popup-like markers still referenced by helper code is safer in M2 than replacing every selector contract at once.

`elementPolicy.ts` and `useMobileInteractions.ts` are in scope only because they are live supporting infrastructure for the same overlay marker surface; this is required completion work, not scope expansion.

Automated verification remains limited to shell and jsdom/Vitest. Any check that requires a running browser session or manual interaction should be reported as `skipped` with executor-notes evidence on first encounter.
